results.transformation_description: Rewrote the functions to use early returns, clearer variable scoping, and for-loop style traversals for the list searches. Logic and order of operations are preserved: the same comparisons, list manipulations, and cleanup steps occur, but with simplified control flow and localized variables, which does not change observable behavior.

equivalence_assessments.justification: All functions preserve control flow, side effects, and return values. Changes are cosmetic or structurally equivalent: early return in free_client_subscription is equivalent to original null check; variable scoping and declaration order changes do not alter behavior; TimerThreadRemove handling and timeout freeing are identical modulo naming; list traversal in freeClientSubList, RemoveClientSubClientSID, GetClientSubClientSID, and GetClientSubActualSID is behaviorally unchanged (loop style and use of temporary variables differ but perform the same comparisons, link updates, deletions, and return the same results). No new branches, side effects, or error cases were introduced.

All five transformed functions preserve the control flow, side effects, and return values of the originals for the same valid inputs. Changes are limited to: (1) early return on NULL in free_client_subscription (equivalent to original guarding the body with if(sub)); (2) reordering of non-dependent operations inside free_client_subscription; (3) renaming of loop variables and restructuring loops as for-loops, which does not alter traversal order or behavior; (4) using explicit == 0 comparisons instead of logical negation on strcmp/memcmp results; and (5) exiting RemoveClientSubClientSID immediately after deleting the found node rather than nulling the iterator and letting the loop condition exit, which is behaviorally identical. No new side effects, no changes to memory management, and no altered conditions are introduced.

All functions preserve control flow, side effects, and return values for valid inputs. Changes are structural and stylistic only: early returns instead of outer if, reordered but independent operations, loop variable renaming and for/while interchange, and using ==0 instead of !strcmp/memcmp. RemoveClientSubClientSID still unlinks the first matching node, frees that node and any following nodes, and updates *head appropriately; breaking vs setting finger=NULL both terminate the loop after handling the match. GetClientSub* functions still return the first matching subscription or NULL. No behavioral differences are introduced for defined inputs.
